networks.name: Impact.com
networks.notes: Second US network — dual listing increases publisher reach

networks.name: CueLinks
networks.notes: International/India market; 4.5% payout as of March 2026

commission.type: flat (US/UK/EU); tiered by product type and volume (Nordics)
commission.notes: 8% consistent flat rate on all US transactions. Nordics program offers higher ceiling (up to 15%) by product type or volume. SOLIX sub-brand runs a separate program at 5%.

creative_assets.notes: Regularly updated banners and text links; special coupons and seasonal promotions available; dedicated affiliate manager accessible

affiliate_program.payment_terms: Standard network payment terms via Rakuten/Impact; no minimum threshold explicitly stated on program page
restrictions: No simulated purchase patterns or scripts

anker_solix.notes: Separate program for Anker's energy/power station sub-brand. Lower commission (5%) reflects higher AOV products ($200–$3,000+).

ankerwork.notes: Workplace/B2B accessories sub-brand; commission details not confirmed in this audit cycle

vs_benchmark.commission: above_median — 8% vs 5% category median; at upper-mid range vs 12% high
vs_benchmark.cookie: at_median — 30 days matches category standard exactly
vs_benchmark.network_coverage: strong — dual-listed on Rakuten + Impact plus regional networks

afare_signals.commission_note: 8% is well above the 5% category median and among the top performers in consumer electronics. Nordics ceiling of 15% puts the brand in elite territory for that region.
afare_signals.cookie_note: 30 days matches the category median exactly. Competitive, though some peers (e.g., longer-window programs) exceed this.
strengths: 8% flat rate significantly above 5% category median — strong EPC signal
Dual-network presence (Rakuten + Impact) maximizes publisher accessibility
Dedicated affiliate manager reduces friction for content partners
Self-serve creative library (banners, data feeds, custom links) enables fast activation
Seasonal coupons and promotions = content hooks for review sites and deal publishers
SuperChargers creator program provides additional pathway for social media affiliates
30-day cookie at category standard; perceived as exceeding norms per Anker's own positioning
Brand trust and product quality reduce pre-sale objections; high conversion rate (~2% stated)
weaknesses: SOLIX sub-brand commission (5%) is lower than main Anker rate — may confuse affiliates if they navigate to wrong program
AnkerWork commission rate unconfirmed — gap in program intelligence
CueLinks international rate (4.5%) is a significant cut vs direct US rate (8%) — channel fragmentation
No publicly stated payment schedule / minimum threshold on program page
Cookie is at median — competitors with 45–60 day windows have an edge for considered purchases
opportunities: High-value Anker SOLIX products ($200–$3K AOV) yield strong EPC even at 5% — worth separate content strategy
Spring/summer seasonal promotions (power banks, outdoor charging) prime for affiliate activation
Creator/SuperCharger program can funnel social media traffic alongside traditional review site affiliates
Nordics 8–15% tiered model could inform a US tiered-volume program upgrade
AI shopping agent compatibility: Anker's data feed availability makes it well-suited for programmatic affiliate placements
threats: Amazon Associates is the default channel for most Anker buyers — affiliate links compete with embedded Amazon placements on review sites
Competing brands (UGREEN 8–15%, RAVPower 8–20%) offer comparable or higher rates
Multiple sub-brand programs (Anker, SOLIX, AnkerWork) risk diluting affiliate attention without clear unified program messaging
Flat US rate (8%) with no volume tier may not incentivize top publishers to prioritize Anker over higher-ceiling alternatives

changes_since_last_audit.changes_detected: No material program changes detected. 8% commission and 30-day cookie appear stable and unchanged. Both Rakuten/LinkShare and Impact.com listings remain active. No rate increases or decreases noted in Q1 2026 sources.
notable_observations: Anker's own page explicitly markets '30-day cookies that exceed industry standards' — consistent messaging with prior audits
Dual-network presence (Rakuten + Impact) confirmed as ongoing strategy, not a new addition
CueLinks international rate (4.5%) lower than direct program — may reflect network margin; consistent with prior data

recommended_actions: Flag Anker as a top-tier affiliate program in consumer electronics for LiveChannel vendors — 8% + 30-day cookie + dual network = strong foundation
Develop SOLIX-specific affiliate content strategy targeting portable power buyers; $200–$3K AOV at 5% = $10–$150 EPC
Confirm AnkerWork commission rate in next audit cycle to complete sub-brand picture
Monitor for any Anker SuperCharger program expansion that could bridge influencer + affiliate channels
Cross-reference with Mac Fly AFARE and Mac Bee ACES for full Flywheel readiness on Anker as a vendor recommendation
